summary: Iteration 5 backend testing complete — 67/67 pytest cases pass (45 carry-over + 22 new iter5). All 4 critical fixes verified live: (1) /api/uploads/<file> serves real binary image content (200 + image/* + 662KB) via the K8s ingress — proven against an actual file on disk; (2) Shopify is GREEN in /api/integrations/health (ok=true, detail mentions auth), and an in-process call to fetch_shopify_products() returns 47 real products from cdn.shopify.com (≥40 required); (3) /api/canva/callback NEVER 422s anymore — no-params, ?error=access_denied, and ?code=x&state=fake_state ALL return 307 redirects to /settings?canva=error&detail=... and each writes a row to db.canva_callback_log with the correct outcome ('missing_code_or_state', 'canva_redirect_with_error', 'invalid_state_not_in_db'); (4) /api/canva/debug returns the documented schema (client_id, configured_redirect_uri, public_backend_url, scopes_requested, token_saved, recent_oauth_states, recent_callbacks). All Canva/Calendar/Posts/Brand/Insights/Auth/Health regression endpoints still green. Per the agent-to-agent note, OAuth tokens remain empty so /templates|/autofill|/create-post correctly 401 — that path was re-verified.

minor.issue: Redirect URLs in the callback handler don't URL-encode the `error` parameter (e.g. `?canva=error&detail={error}` interpolates raw). Canva always sends OAuth2-spec error codes (`access_denied`, `invalid_request`, etc.) which are URL-safe, so it's fine in practice — but if Canva ever returns a value with `&`/space the frontend would mis-parse. Defensive `urllib.parse.quote(error)` would harden it.

On invalid_state path, the handler does NOT delete the OAuth state row even though it logs the attempt. This is correct (state may still be legitimate and just stale), but worth noting that orphaned canva_oauth_states rows will accumulate forever. Consider a TTL index on `created_at` (e.g. 30 min) for canva_oauth_states.

fetch_shopify_products() only requests up to `limit` products in a single page (no pagination). Today the store has 47 — fits in one page with limit=250 — but if catalog grows >250, callers that rely on the full list (guardrail price match) will silently miss new SKUs.

assetify() rewrites /uploads/ -> /api/uploads/ but does NOT rewrite already-prefixed /api/uploads/ paths nor handle relative paths without leading slash. Current callers always pass leading-slash paths so it works — flag only.

action_items: (Optional, hardening) URL-encode `error` and `state` values in the Canva callback redirect URLs using urllib.parse.quote() — protects against any future non-URL-safe values from Canva.

(Optional, ops hygiene) Add a TTL index on db.canva_oauth_states.created_at (e.g. 1800 sec) so orphaned state rows from abandoned OAuth attempts auto-expire. Same for canva_callback_log if it grows large (suggest 30-day TTL since it's a debug log).
(Optional, scaling) Add pagination to fetch_shopify_products() when total > 250 — use page_info link header from Shopify's /admin/api/2024-01/products.json?limit=250 to follow next pages.

(Reminder) User still needs to complete the Canva OAuth click-through. Until then /api/canva/templates|/autofill|/create-post correctly 401. The callback + debug + log infrastructure is in place and ready to diagnose the next OAuth attempt.
critical_code_review_comments: Static asset routing fix is verified end-to-end: server.py:54-57 dual-mounts UPLOADS_DIR at BOTH /uploads (local) and /api/uploads (ingress-reachable). GET https://.../api/uploads/<existing>.png returns 200 / image/png / 662322 bytes with valid binary magic bytes. The frontend assetUrl() rewrite + backend agents.assetify() rewrite both target /api/uploads/, so external services (Postproxy) and the frontend will resolve them.

Canva callback handler at canva.py:156-239 is correctly signed with `request: Request` + `Optional[str] = Query(default=None)` for code/state/error/error_description — the previous 422 root cause (FastAPI rejecting missing required Query) is gone. Every branch (error, missing_params, invalid_state, token_exchange_failed, exception, success) inserts into db.canva_callback_log with a distinct `outcome` field before returning RedirectResponse — diagnosability is excellent.

/api/canva/debug at canva.py:241-261 returns all 7 documented keys + a `token_meta` extra. It correctly excludes `code_verifier` from oauth_states (sensitive PKCE secret) and excludes access_token/refresh_token from the token doc — leak-safe by design.

fetch_shopify_products() now returns real data: live in-process call retrieved 47 products with cdn.shopify.com images (verified). Falls back to _mock_products() (5 items) only when SHOPIFY_TOKEN/URL missing or HTTP fails — correct defensive behavior. Static check confirmed X-Shopify-Access-Token header usage.

Code-quality nit (carried from iter4): canva.py opens an httpx.AsyncClient inside each poll iteration of _poll_autofill_job and export_design_png — minor perf, no functional impact. Could be hoisted to one client per call.

Test isolation observation: the in-process Shopify test (test_fetch_shopify_products_real) required explicit load_dotenv('/app/backend/.env') + importlib.reload(agents) because agents.py reads env vars at module-import time. Backend process loads .env via supervisor so this is only a test-time concern.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iteration5.py (NEW — 22 tests covering all 4 iter5 fixes: uploads static serving, Shopify GREEN + live ≥40 products, Canva callback no-422 with 3 outcome paths, /api/canva/debug schema, plus regression on /api/canva/{status,connect,templates,autofill,create-post,disconnect}, /api/auth/login, /api/integrations/health, /api/posts, /api/calendar, /api/brand, /api/competitors, /api/insights)

/app/backend/tests/test_backend.py (regex on line 386 updated to accept /uploads/ OR /api/uploads/ — iter5 fix #1 made canva.export_design_png() return /api/uploads/<fname> instead of /uploads/<fname>; the test was stale)
seed_data_creation: None. The 3 canva callback hits in test_debug_logs_callbacks did insert 3 rows into db.canva_callback_log with TEST_iter5 markers in their state field — they are diagnostic-only and harmless (the /api/canva/debug endpoint just shows last 10).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Two test files now live: /app/backend/tests/test_backend.py (45 tests, iter1-4) and /app/backend/tests/test_iteration5.py (22 tests, iter5). Run both with: cd /app && python -m pytest backend/tests/ -v. The in-process Shopify test (TestShopifyProducts.test_fetch_shopify_products_real) uses load_dotenv + importlib.reload(agents) to pick up .env at runtime — if the agents module is restructured, that import pattern may need updating. The Canva callback log tests rely on Mongo writes being flushed quickly (0.5s sleep) — bump if you see flaky 'no callbacks recorded'. Once user completes Canva OAuth, the /templates and /create-post tests that currently assert 401 'Canva not connected' will need to be upgraded to live happy-path tests with a real brand_template_id and an assertion that the returned image_url starts with `/api/uploads/canva_` (export success) OR matches the Canva thumbnail URL (fallback). Also assert post.canva.exported_png ∈ {`/api/uploads/...` string, None}.
rca of the issue:
No backend bugs found. All 4 fixes verified:

1. **Static asset serving** — server.py:54-57 dual-mount StaticFiles at /uploads (local) AND /api/uploads (ingress). curl -i https://.../api/uploads/<file>.png → HTTP 200, content-type: image/png, content-length 662322. The frontend assetUrl() (api.js) + backend assetify() (agents.py:172-183) both rewrite legacy /uploads/ → /api/uploads/, so all downstream consumers (Postproxy media_urls, FE img src, Canva exports) hit a reachable URL.

2. **Shopify GREEN** — Fresh shpat_ token (prefix shpat_3e769e) authenticates against https://ajy6hu-sh.myshopify.com/admin/api/2024-01/products.json — returns 200 with 47 products carrying cdn.shopify.com image URLs. /api/integrations/health.shopify.ok=true; detail mentions 'Authenticated'.

3. **Canva callback never 422** — canva.py:157-163 signature uses Request + Optional[str] = Query(default=None). Three live calls verified:
   - GET /api/canva/callback → 307 → /settings?canva=error&detail=missing_params (log outcome='missing_code_or_state')
   - GET /api/canva/callback?error=access_denied → 307 → /settings?canva=error&detail=access_denied (log outcome='canva_redirect_with_error')
   - GET /api/canva/callback?code=test&state=fake_state_TEST → 307 → /settings?canva=error&detail=invalid_state (log outcome='invalid_state_not_in_db')
   /api/canva/debug confirmed all 3 outcomes are persisted with full_url + query_params + timestamps.

4. **/api/canva/debug** — Returns all 7 required keys with correct values: client_id=OC-AZ7fmu8hwU0b, configured_redirect_uri=https://bagnshop-ai-build-1.preview.emergentagent.com/api/canva/callback, public_backend_url matches, scopes_requested includes brandtemplate/design/asset scopes, token_saved=false (expected pre-OAuth), recent_oauth_states[] and recent_callbacks[] arrays populated.

Two test failures during authoring were SCRIPT bugs, not code bugs: (a) PNG magic-bytes assertion was too strict — some files on disk were saved as .png but contain JPEG bytes (carried over from external image gen); relaxed to accept any binary image magic + size>100KB. (b) The in-process Shopify call returned mock data because agents.py reads SHOPIFY_ADMIN_TOKEN at module-import time and pytest doesn't load .env automatically — fixed by load_dotenv() + importlib.reload(agents).
